description_long: This is a large, cylindrical barrel with a weathered, yellowish exterior and a corroded, reddish-brown top surface, suggesting exposure to harsh elements. The top appears sealed and stained, with a warning label and a prominent radioactive hazard symbol on the side, indicating it is used for storing or transporting hazardous materials. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible grime and irregularities, especially near the top edge where corrosion is most pronounced. The barrel lacks visible handles or reinforcement bands, giving it a simple, industrial appearance. Its size and markings suggest it is intended for secure containment in restricted or hazardous urban environments. The overall construction appears to be thick-walled, likely metal, designed to prevent leaks or breaches.
description: A large, weathered yellow barrel with a corroded top and radioactive warning symbol, used for hazardous material storage. The surface is rough and industrial, with no visible handles or reinforcements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radioactive symbol and corroded top edge clearly.
description_view_1: Side view with the radioactive symbol partially visible and more of the corroded top surface exposed.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the radioactive symbol in full view and the warning label on the top more visible.
description_view_3: Angled view with the radioactive symbol partially visible and the corroded top edge prominent.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: radioactive symbol, warning label on top
